Countless castles

Much like our list of London landmarks, there are too many castles in Wales to describe in detail. There are over 600 in total, giving the country more castle than anywhere else in the world per square mile. That said, they range from ruins to majestic masterpieces which have been kept completely intact.  It’s hard for anyone to argue that ruins are a famous landmark. So, here are our top contenders for landmark castles in Wales:
  • Caernarfon Castle
  • Conwy Castle
  • Harlech Castle
  • Pembroke Castle
  • Chepstow Castle
  • Chrik Castle
  • Skenfrith Castle
  • Whitecastle
  • Raglan Castle
  • Caerphilly Castle
  • Cardiff Castle
  • Powis Castle
 

See famous landmarks in style

Between them, England and Wales have plenty of famous landmarks to enjoy. With kate & tom’s, you can see all the best landmarks in style with our selection of luxury holiday cottages. Whether you’re looking for a coastal cottage in Wales for your trip to Snowdon or a house with a hot tub just a stone’s throw from Stonehenge, you’ll find it in our collection. Need a hand with your search? Simply email hello@kateandtoms.com and we’ll point you in the right direction or answer any questions you may have.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: 9 Famous Landmarks in England & Wales | kate & tom’s
There are plenty of famous landmarks to choose from in England and Wales. Discover some of the best, from Durdle Door and Stonehenge to Mount Snowdon.

item.title: Best Toddler Holidays UK (2-3 Year Olds)
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
Planning a holiday with toddlers is very exciting. Not only will you get the chance to create memories to last a lifetime, but it also gives you and your youngster the chance to explore the wonderful UK. Whether they love running around in the countryside or building sandcastles on the beach, there’s so much to do - and looking into the best toddler holidays UK wide can help you find the perfect spot for your family adventure. Of course, going on holidays with all the family also comes with a unique set of challenges. Kids aged between two and three are generally little balls of energy, which means it’s essential to plan a getaway that provides plenty to keep them entertained. For family breaks by the sea, kate & tom’s luxury Cornwall cottages offer plenty of space, style and toddler-friendly fun near the coast. – which come complete with swimming pools, soft play and travel cots – you'll find a wide range of amenities suitable for young children in the UK. So, if you are looking to spend quality time with the entire family without the worry of entertainment, you're in for a treat... At kate & tom’s, we understand that travelling with toddlers requires a unique approach. That's why we’ve curated a list of the best baby and toddler-friendly holidays, featuring destinations across the UK. As well as location ideas, we’ve thrown in a wide range of toddler-friendly attractions the whole family can enjoy. All you need to do is pack the car!  

Sandcastles and surf: Family-friendly holidays in Cornwall

Blue Flag beaches and nostalgia-soaked seaside towns make Cornwall one of the best holidays for 2-year-olds, babies and older children. Challenge your little ones to a sandcastle-building competition on the beach before cooling off with a splash in the surf. Not to mention beautiful coastal walks to discover, including lots of pram-friendly options and outdoor play areas.  Big-ticket attractions like the Eden Project promise a fun-filled day, rain or shine. Does your toddler love animals? Take them to meet animals at Newquay Zoo and discover vibrant underwater ecosystems at Blue Reef Aquarium. There's also plenty of other local attractions you can visit while you're there. When it’s time to regroup and recharge, return to your cosy luxury Cornwall cottage for some well-deserved peace and quiet. Put the kids down for a nap and relax out on the terrace. A glass of wine in the sunshine or a dip in the pool is the perfect way to finish your day.   

Hunt for fossils in Dorset

Dorset’s Jurassic Coast isn’t just a UNESCO World Heritage Site. It’s also one of the best places in the world to hunt for fossils. Pint-sized explorers will love combing local beaches near Charmouth and Lyme Regis in search of ancient relics.  When you’ve had your fix of palaeontology, head to Monkey World in Wareham to come face-to-face with cheeky primates. Or, if your little ones prefer playing in the sea, we'd recommend checking out Weymouth Beach for fun in the (hopefully) sun.  Once you've worn yourself and your little explorers out, bring the crew back to your Dorset holiday cottage and wind down for the evening. To keep your children's bedtime routine, you can pre-book baby baths, changing mats, travel cots, bed guards and high chairs. If you want some extra safety, you can also request stair gates. Dorset is a great place to make unforgettable memories with the whole family.   

A baby and toddler-friendly coastal escape to Pembrokeshire

If you prefer rugged coastal seascapes over rolling hills, you’ll love Pembrokeshire in Wales. Check into a luxury cottage near the coast and enjoy windswept beach walks and cosy nights by the fireplace. Watch as your toddler explores the breathtaking surroundings of one of the UK's most beautiful coasts.  Why not start your day before breakfast? You can grab a bite at a quaint little café in town. Then, once you're all fuelled up and the baby is fed, take a walk to the park and check out the local attractions and playgrounds. If the weather isn't playing ball, there are indoor soft play centres you can book to keep the children entertained while the parents have a cuppa. If you want to go that extra mile, attractions like Pembroke Castle promise hours of entertainment for high-energy toddlers. It’s one of the best-preserved Norman castles in Wales and features a maze of tunnels, passages and stairways just waiting to be discovered. The combination of outdoor activities and family-friendly attractions makes Pembrokeshire one of the best baby and toddler holidays in the UK for 2-year-olds.  

Discover the magic of Beatrix Potter in the Lake District

The Lake District has enchanted artists and authors for centuries, including beloved English writer Beatrix Potter. Kids and parents love the World of Beatrix Potter in Bowness-on-Windermere, which brings the magic of characters like Peter Rabbit to life.  Beyond Beatrix Potter, the Lakes offer plenty of outdoor activities to keep little ones busy. Enjoy scenic lakeside strolls, go on a Gruffalo hunt in the forest, or set sail on a boat cruise and explore the fantastic adventure playground at Lowther Castle. If you want a day to chill, check out one of the local soft play centres or swimming baths.  But that's not all; even after your day of discovery has ended, the fun continues when returning to one of our Lake District holiday homes. Once the kiddies are fed and everyone's in bed, indulge yourself with a dip in the hot tub or enjoy the space the cottage's large swimming pool. The Lake District is a fantastic destination for young families.   

Explore storybook villages in the Cotswolds

From Bibury and Bourton-on-the-Water to Cirencester and Chipping Campden, the Cotswolds is dotted with storybook towns and villages. The region is famous for outdoor playing areas and ice cream, making it one of the best holidays for 3-year-olds and babies. For parents, treats like Gloucester Old Spot pork, Bibury trout and Stinking Bishop cheese will have your mouth watering.  Budding animal enthusiasts love meeting giraffes, rhinos and other exotic species at the Cotswold Wildlife Park and Gardens. If you've decided you want to take a break from the norm, why not take the kids to the Diddly Squat Farm Shop – the location of the hit TV show Clarkson's Farm? Enjoy the beautiful scenery and pick up some fresh produce.  Fancy some downtime? Travelling with a baby and toddler is no easy feat, and sometimes you might want to find some peace and quiet at your holiday abode. Relax and put your feet up at a Cotswolds holiday home – it's ideal if you each want your own space and time to re-live the day.   

Adventure awaits in Somerset

Somerset is another one of the best baby-friendly holidays in the UK. Perfect for toddlers and older children too, why not take a walk on the wild side at Longleat Safari Park? Home to more than 120 species, the drive-through safari park allows you to get up close with cheetahs, giraffes, monkeys, and more. The driving element makes this a fantastic activity for younger kids. Little ones will be in their element at Wookey Hole Caves, a unique family attraction that showcases a hidden subterranean world. If you fancy doing something different during your family holiday to Somerset, we'd recommend taking the children to The Fleet Air Arm Museum – home to some of the most famous aircraft in the world. You can all learn about naval aviation while also meeting some of the volunteers.  

Urban fun: the best baby-friendly holidays in London

What better place to entertain a baby and toddler than London? Chef-driven restaurants come complete with changing tables and high chairs, while trendy bars aren’t the only draw in the capital. However, if you can secure a babysitter for the night, you’re free to explore this side of the city. London is one of the top destinations for baby and toddler holidays, thanks to its abundance of family-friendly activities, including a diverse range of museums in the West End, soft play centres, outdoor play areas in the South, and even outdoor swimming pools in Hampstead Heath. Be amazed by majestic African lions at London Zoo and watch sharks, turtles and rays cruise through crystalline tanks at SEA LIFE London Aquarium. For an adorable afternoon pre-book the Paddington experience and explore the Pawprint Trail. It celebrates the much-loved children’s book character, Paddington Bear, and takes you past a series of charming sights and attractions. Finish with a trip to Hamleys, the legendary London toy store, where your little one can purchase their very own Paddington Bear. Whatever your day includes, you and all the family will have a great time in London, you'll be planning your next family holiday before you even come home! London isn't all hustle and bustle. There's also plenty of space to relax at larger properties just outside of the capital. Reserve your stair gates, bed guards, changing mats, baby baths and any other baby and toddler paraphernalia you might need to make your stay even more enjoyable.   

Toddler-friendly activities on the Northumberland coast

If you want to escape reality, Northumberland is a top choice for a family-friendly holiday in the UK. Head to the peaceful Bamburgh beach, which stretches from Bamburgh castle down to Seahouses, providing ample space to find your little corner for your baby or toddler to enjoy. From Seahouses, you can take a boat trip around the Farne Islands, where you’ll see a variety of wildlife, including puffins and seals. If you’re travelling with older children, be sure to stop by Alnwick Castle nearby too. The majestic building was used as Hogwarts in the first two Harry Potter films, most notably the lesson where Harry learns to fly his broomstick! Northumberland is a baby and toddler-friendly holiday destination that all the family will enjoy. From beautiful forest adventures to long walks to a historic site, you're little ones (and you) will have a ball.   

Plan a toddler-friendly staycation with kate & tom’s

Travelling with toddlers doesn’t mean you have to compromise on comfort, if anything, it's a good chance to have a change of scenery and enjoy some quality time together. At kate & tom’s, we’ve selected a range of luxury, toddler-friendly holiday cottages designed to meet the needs of young families with toddlers and babies. Our large cottages feature spacious living areas, separate bedrooms, well-equipped kitchens and private gardens, making it easy to maintain your routine while you’re away. You can also pre-book baby and toddler-friendly facilities such as high chairs, stair gates and travel cots.  After a day of sightseeing with toddlers in tow, there’s nothing more relaxing than returning to a home away from home. If you have a water baby or a toddler learning to swim, why not book one of our homes with a swimming pool?  If you need any help finding toddler-friendly holidays in the UK, we have a team of staycation experts on hand to help. Email hello@kateandtoms.com today to discuss your needs and learn more about planning the best holidays for toddlers.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Best Toddler Holidays UK: Family Adventures Await | kate & tom's
Discover perfect family adventures with the best toddler holidays the UK has to offer, from countryside retreats to beach fun.

item.title: 10 Must-Haves for a Winter Village Cottage
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
What better way to celebrate winter than with a cosy getaway with friends and family? From drifting snowflakes to twinkling Christmas markets, winter can be a magical time of the year. Whether you live in the city and are dreaming of an escape to the countryside, or you’re already based in a small town or village but looking for a change of scene, winter village cottages provide the perfect base.

When browsing properties, there are certain features that can take your experience to the next level. At kate & tom's, we cherry-pick the best luxury holiday rentals in the UK, and we know exactly what makes a winter village cottage truly exceptional. Looking for inspiration? Read on for our guide to the top 10 must-haves for a winter village cottage.

1. The perfect location

Location is everything when it comes to choosing the perfect winter village cottage. While some destinations like the Cornish coast shine in the summer, places like the Cotswolds are just as enchanting in the winter months. The region is dotted with chocolate-box villages that provide a dreamy backdrop for your winter holiday. Whether it’s a beautifully restored Georgian manor in Oxfordshire or a stylish barn conversion in in Somerset, you’ll find properties for every occasion in the Cotswolds.

As well as invigorating winter walks, there are plenty of indoor activities to keep you entertained in the Cotswolds. Visit heritage properties like Snowshill Manor, hunt for treasures at antique shops in towns like Witney and treat yourself to lavish afternoon teas. Remember, it’s not a proper Cotswolds afternoon tea without freshly baked scones, homemade preserves and lashings of clotted cream.

2. Proximity to a local pub

What’s a country getaway without a pint or two at a cosy local pub? Being able to stroll along a snow-dusted lane to an inviting pub is one of the joys of staying in a small village. While you’re probably want to cook at least few meals in your chef’s kitchen, nothing compares to a piping hot Sunday roast complete with Yorkshire puddings, crispy roast potatoes and a glass of Bordeaux to really set off the rich flavours.

You’ll find some beautiful cottages within walking distance of local pubs in the Lake District, given that Cumbria has more microbreweries than any other UK county. The region also happens to be the birthplace of beloved British dessert, sticky toffee pudding – bonus!

3. Indoor pool

British winters can be icy but that doesn’t mean you can’t make a splash on your country getaway. Book a cottage with a heated indoor pool and you can take the plunge in rain, shine, snow and whatever else the weather throws at you. Imagine starting your day with a leisurely swim while watching snowflakes drift down outside. Absolute bliss.

4. Log fire

Nothing says “winter getaway” like a crackling log fire. When temperatures plummet and wild weather rolls in, a log fire ensures your village cottage feels warm and welcoming. There's nothing quite like curling up with a good book, a mug of Belgian hot chocolate and a cosy cashmere blanket in the morning. After dinner, retreat to the fireside to enjoy a glass of wine and good conversation.

5. Outdoor hot tub

After a breath-taking walk in the countryside there’s no better way to warm up than with a soak in a private outdoor hot tub. No need to bundle up in your warmest winter clothes and wool blankets to enjoy the fresh air and wintry landscapes. Simply strip off and plunge into a steaming hot tub. Don’t forget a bottle of fizz!

6. A well-equipped kitchen

Whether you’re into Jamie, Nigella or Ottolenghi, winter is the perfect time to get creative in the kitchen. Book a winter village cottage with a well-equipped kitchen and you can whip up hearty, homemade meals at your leisure. From Middle Eastern inspired brunches to classic winter warmers like shepherd's pie and beef stew, you’ll love how easy it is to cook up delicious feasts in a chef’s kitchen.

7. Games room

A games room is a terrific addition to any winter cottage, especially if you're traveling with family or friends. Challenge each other to a game of pool, get competitive with a table tennis tournament and wind up a family-style meal with a game of darts and whisky on the rocks.

8. Underfloor heating

Say goodbye to cold feet and book a winter village cottage with underfloor heating. No need to stoke the fireplace first thing in the morning. Instead, simply roll out of bed and onto toasty warm floors. Underfloor heating is a small luxury but can make a big difference when it comes to winter escapes in the UK.

9. Stunning views

From frost-covered fields to snow-dusted forests, winter holidays in the UK can be beautiful. Choose a country cottage with stunning views and you won’t even need to venture outside to enjoy the winter wonderland.

10. Luxury touches

Don't underestimate the importance of luxury touches when booking a winter village cottage. Unlike summer escapes, you’ll probably be spending a lot more time indoors. This means large cottages with luxuries like plush sofas, feather-soft throws, down duvets and tasteful décor can make all the difference in creating a cosy setting for your winter getaway.

11. Book your cosy winter village cottage getaway today

Keep these must-haves in mind and you’re ten steps closer to finding the perfect winter village cottage. From the best location in a charming village to luxury amenities like indoor pools and hot tubs, these features will ensure your winter escape lives up to all your expectations, and more.

Need more assistance finding the winter village cottage you’ve been dreaming of? Email hello@kateandtoms.com and we’ll help you book a property that ticks all your boxes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: 10 Must-Haves for a Winter Village Cottage | kate & tom’s
Check out our 10 must-have features for a winter village cottage – from the perfect location to luxury amenities like indoor pools, hot tubs and games rooms

item.title: Supersize Your Getaway with a Large Family Cottage
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to planning group getaways, there’s something special about having everyone under one roof. This is where large family holiday cottages come into play. Designed to host big groups of 30+ people, they offer the space, comfort and amenities that hotels simply can’t compete with. Whether you’re planning multigenerational family celebrations or a getaway with dozens of your closest friends, this guide is for you.

We'll explore the advantages of choosing large family holiday homes over alternative options, as well as the perks of opting for a UK retreat as opposed to travelling abroad. We’ll also provide insider tips on how to book the perfect group-friendly property that ticks all your boxes.

Advantages of a large cottage versus alternatives

A sense of togetherness

One of the key benefits of renting a large family holiday cottage is the ability to keep your entire group together. Communal living spaces ensure there are plenty of opportunities to socialise, without the need to coordinate plans across separate accommodations. Whether you’re sipping a bottle of Bordeaux by the fireplace, cooking up a feast in the chef’s kitchen or enjoying a movie night in the private cinema, big holiday rentals bring that all-important sense of togetherness to your getaway.

Cost-effective

Large family holiday homes often offer exceptional value for money compared to booking multiple rooms at a hotel, guesthouse or B&B. You can split the cost of the property between the group or just get a great deal if accommodation is your treat.

Convenience

Forget the logistics of coordinating arrivals and departures across various locations. With a private residence, you can all arrive at the same place, on the same day, at the same time. What better way to kickstart a group getaway?

Privacy

Large family holiday cottages or luxury manor houses offer plenty of opportunities to socialise. But thanks to separate bedrooms, ensuites and other intimate spaces, you’ll never feel short on privacy. For example, get up early for a relaxing sunrise swim in the outdoor swimming pool or keep things social with morning mimosas in the hot tub.

Fun and games

Whether it’s a tennis tournament on your private court (complete with Pimm’s, strawberries and cream) or an action-packed night in the games room, large family holiday cottages offer the amenities you need to entertain large groups. When you’ve booked your property and know what facilities are available, don’t hesitate to get creative with your itinerary and plan all kinds of fun and games.

Advantages of a large cottage in England vs. travelling abroad

Accessibility

A large family holiday home in England makes it much easier for everyone to attend your gathering, especially if you have a multigenerational group. There's less travel time and fewer logistics to consider.

Reduced admin

Booking big family holidays in England typically comes with less administrative hassle. You're dealing with familiar systems, language and currency, making the planning and booking process more straightforward.

Diverse landscapes

Just because you’re holidaying in the UK, it doesn’t mean you can’t experience new and exciting landscapes. From coastal retreats to remote countryside hideaways, you'll find luxury cottages and spacious farmhouses to suit all tastes and travel styles.

Access to attractions

Many large rental properties in England and Wales are just a stone’s throw from popular attractions and historic sites. This makes it easy to plan day trips, outings and activities that will appeal to everyone in your group.

 

Celebrate in style with large family holiday cottages

Spacious cottages aren’t just for big family holidays, though they do provide a perfect backdrop for spending quality time with loved ones. From New Year’s Eve getaways to anniversary celebrations and friendship retreats, luxury holiday rentals cater to all kinds of special occasions.

For example, a beautifully renovated Victorian manor house in the heart of the Cotswolds could be the perfect setting for your next family reunion. Share stories with grandparents in elegant living spaces, watch the little ones play in the flower-filled garden and curl up by the fireplace with a good book.

Or why not escape to the seaside and experience the magic of Cornwall in a charming barn conversion? Perfect for group summer holidays, you’ll have sandy beaches, scenic walking trails and family-friendly historic sites at your doorstep.

Tips on finding the perfect large holiday cottage

With so many amazing properties to choose from, there’s a perfect space for every occasion. All you have to do is find it! Here’s some tips from our in-house team on how to find a property that matches the unique needs of your group.

Define your requirements

Start by defining the size and specific features you need for your big family holiday property. Consider the number of bedrooms, bathrooms and common areas needed to comfortably accommodate your group.

Location, location, location

Think about the type of location that best suits your group's needs and interests, as well as the season. Whether you want a coastal escape, a countryside retreat or easy access to cultural attractions, choose a location that aligns with your vacation goals.

Amenities and facilities

Pay attention to the cottage's amenities. For example, does it offer a swimming pool, hot tub or games room? Make a list of must-haves for your large family holiday and use it to create a shortlist.

Reviews and recommendations

Read reviews from previous guests to get an idea of the overall experience offered by the property. Recommendations from friends or family who have stayed in large cottages can also be invaluable.

Budget considerations

Set a budget for your large family holiday property, including rental costs any additional fees. Keep in mind that some properties may require a security deposit.

Flexible booking options

Consider whether the property offers flexible booking options, especially if your travel plans may change.

Book your dream large family holiday

Comparing large family holiday cottages doesn’t have to be difficult. It should be fun! At kate & tom’s, we’ve made things easier by selecting the finest large family holiday homes in stunning locations across England and Wales.

Need any more help? Simply let us know your unique needs and personal preferences, and we’ll do our best to find the perfect property. Or why not check out our luxurious new houses, freshly added to our holiday property list?  Email hello@kateandtoms.com to get started.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Supersize Your Getaway with a Large Family Cottage | kate & toms
Go big with our complete guide on supersizing your next getaway with a large family holiday cottage – including the many advantages of a UK holiday home.

item.title: 10 of the Best Museums in the UK
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
Whether you’re a lifelong art aficionado or looking for ways to entertain the kids on your next holiday, you’ll find museums for every age and interest in the UK. While London boasts some world-renowned institutions, we're here to shine a light on some of the other lesser known museums scattered across England and Wales. From the rolling hills of the Cotswolds to the historic city of Cardiff, read on to discover 10 of the best museums in the UK.

1. The Jorvik Viking Centre – York

Step back in time and discover England’s fierce history at the Jorvik Viking Centre in York. This immersive museum transports you to the 10th century and offers a unique chance to experience the Viking-age city of Jorvik. Walk around reconstructed buildings, discover ancient archaeological relics and admire life-size dioramas. Costumed interpreters tie the experience together and offer a unique glimpse of the Viking world.

2. The Roman Baths – Bath

Set in the picturesque city of Bath, this UNESCO World Heritage Site protects more than 2000 years of history. The site is one of the best-preserved ancient bathhouses in the world and features walkways that allow you to explore areas like the Sacred Spring and Roman Temple on foot.

Next to the site is an engaging museum filled with treasures from Roman Britain. Minerva's head, a gilt bronze sculpture dedicated to the goddess of healing, is the museum showpiece. Pair your visit with afternoon tea at the beautifully restored Pump Room Restaurant. Don’t miss the chance to try mineral-rich water from the fountain, which has been coveted for its therapeutic properties for millennia.

 

3. The Natural History Museum – London

Okay, we said no London. But no roundup of the best museums in UK towns and cities would be complete without a nod to the Natural History Museum in the capital. From the towering Diplodocus skeleton in the main hall to the shimmering rocks, minerals, gemstones and geodes in the geology halls, the famous museum delights science enthusiasts of all ages.

4. The Ashmolean Museum – Oxford

Oxford's Ashmolean Museum is one of the first public museums in the UK. It was founded in the 1600s and stands proudly on Beaumont Street, one of the city’s main thoroughfares. Inside you can discover an extensive art and archaeology collection, ranging from Egyptian mummies to European masterpieces. The museum's rooftop restaurant commands sweeping views over the Oxford skyline.

5. The Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre – Great Missenden

For a family-friendly day out, head to the Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre in Buckinghamshire, the hometown of beloved British author. The museum celebrates the life and legacy of Roald Dahl with interactive exhibits inspired by classics like Charlie and the Chocolate Factory.

6. The Big Pit National Coal Museum – Blaenavon, Wales

Descend deep underground at The Big Pit National Coal Museum in Blaenavon, Wales. The former coal mine is reimagined as a unique attraction, complete with guided tours led by ex-miners. If you’re interested in the industrial heritage of Wales, it’s easily amongst the best museums in the UK.

Looking for more museum-hopping inspiration in Wales? Set in the grounds of a centuries-old castle, the St Fagans National Museum of History takes a deep dive into the Welsh narrative. It’s located in a village just outside Cardiff, making it a great way to break up a day of city sightseeing in the Welsh capital. The open-air style of this museum means it’s best visited in the sunshine.

7. The Imperial War Museum North – Manchester

Located in Manchester, the Imperial War Museum North is an architectural marvel designed by acclaimed Polish-American architect, Daniel Libeskind. Clad in gleaming aluminium and set on the waterfront, it’s impossible to miss. Thought-provoking exhibits explore how conflicts like WWI have affected society over the years.

8. The Royal Pavilion – Brighton

The Royal Pavilion in Brighton is a true feast for the eyes. The former royal pleasure palace combines an eclectic mix of architectural and interior décor styles, drawing inspiration from Europe, India, China and beyond. The result is incredible and dazzles kids and adults alike. Speaking of little ones, there’s plenty to keep younger visitors entertained at the Royal Pavilion, whether it’s exploring the lavish rooms, running around the Regency-style gardens or taking part in hands-on workshops, activities and storytelling sessions.

9. The Royal Cornwall Museum – Truro

Lip-smacking cream teas aren’t the only draw in Cornwall. The region also boasts some fantastic cultural attractions, including the Royal Cornwall Museum in Truro. A celebration of Cornish history and culture, the museum showcases everything from fine art at ancient relics. It's the perfect addition to any Cornish getaway.

10.Cotswold Motoring Museum – Bourton-on-the-Water

Tucked away in the picturesque village of Bourton-on-the-Water, the Cotswold Motoring Museum is a paradise for petrol heads. Learn about the evolution of British motoring history and admire an impressive collection of vintage cars, including icons like the Mini Cooper. Interactive exhibits and plenty of hands-on activities make this museum a top pick for families. While you’re in the area, be sure to treat yourself to Cotswold treats like Gloucester Old Spot pork, Stinking Bishop cheese and lashings of Tewkesbury mustard.

Plan your museum-hopping adventure with kate & tom’s

Whether you’re dreaming of a seaside escape to Sussex or an adventure-packed trip to Wales, accommodation plays an important role in your holiday experience. At kate & tom's, we specialise in luxurious holiday rentals in regions across the UK, making it easy to plan your museum-hopping adventure.

From stately mansions in Cornwall to elegant country estates near Bath, to rural escapes in Buckinghamshire, we have stunning options near all the best museums in the UK. Once you’re done marvelling at motors or embarking on Viking venture, you can retreat to the ultimate base with luxury facilities. We’re talking about spacious living areas, swimming pools, cinema rooms – everything you want and need.

Browse our collection independently or email hello@kateandtoms.com for help tracking down your dream property.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: 10 of the Best Museums in the UK | kate & tom’s
Discover 10 of the best museums in the UK, from Viking ventures in York to a motoring museum in the rolling hills of the Cotswolds.

item.title: The Complete Hen Party Holiday Guide
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
If you want the ultimate celebration for a bride-to-be, it doesn’t get better than a hen party holiday before the wedding. To make things easier, we’ve put together a complete guide, including the big plus points, some hen party holiday ideas and everything you need to know about timings.

Why go on a hen party holiday?

It’s a good question – and one you should certainly be asking as a bride-to-be, bridesmaid or just designated party planner! More specifically, why should you opt for a hen party holiday over the more conventional options?

More time for fun

Firstly, a holiday stops the hen party being short-lived. We’ve all been to hen parties, (or a stag party) which fly by in an instant. You spend months looking forward to it only for the night to pass in an instant. With a hen party holiday, you can get away for a few days rather than a few hours, making far more memories in the process.

Something for everyone

Additionally, hen party holidays provide a lot more variety than a night on the town. Don’t forget that hen parties involve people from all age groups. At a holiday home, everyone can do their own thing while still taking part in the celebrations. Older relatives can retreat to their private quarters for the night if things get too lively, for example, rather than having to miss out altogether.

Don’t forget that you can also book a hen party holiday in a location with lively nightlife if you want to get the best of both worlds!

Mingle to your heart’s content

Finally, a hen party holiday provides much better mingling than any of the alternatives. The bride has more time to see all of her favourite people, rather than having to hastily circulate around the group to make sure nobody is left out. More often than not, those different groups of friends and relatives become one big unit on a hen party holiday.

6 hen party holiday ideas

Below, we’ll look through some great things to do on your hen party holiday…

Pool party

Holiday homes with a private pool are undoubtedly one of the most popular choices for hen parties. With good reason too. It’s a fun activity before the night-time celebrations – not to mention a good spot to recover the following day.

Book a private chef

Food can be one of the highlights of any hen party. But it can also be hard to organise when you want to delight all the attendees. A private chef ensures that the food is unforgettable while adding that extra luxury factor to the getaway.

Cinema night

Who doesn’t love a cinema night? With a private cinema room, you can gather all the girls for a chick flick or just the bride’s favourite movie. Set the scene with a romantic film on the first night or recover from a hangover with a comedy later on in your break.

Hot tub holiday

Hot tub houses are another big draw for hen parties. The bride will love the combination of bubbles and bubbly as everyone toasts her and wishes her well.

Themed fancy dress

Fancy dress is always fun for hen parties. But you might not like the attention it gains in a public place. There’s no such problem at a luxury party house. Everyone can dress as the bride or choose a theme that she loves, then party through the night.

Spa treatments

Spa treatments have become synonymous with hen parties. But you don’t have to plan your entire event at a spa to enjoy them. Mobile spa experiences can be booked for your holiday home, giving all the guests some much-needed pampering in your chosen surroundings.

Timing for a hen party holiday

Ready to start planning? Let’s run through some important timings to consider…

How long should we book?

The great thing about a hen party holiday is that it’s not over in an instant. Shorter breaks are usually 2-3 days, like a long weekend. However, it’s not unusual to have a hen party lasting a full week if everyone has the time.

What dates to choose

Long gone are the days where hen parties were held the night before the wedding. But you don’t want the event to be too long before the big day. Most hen parties are held between 1-2 months prior to the nuptials.

When to book your holiday

With the high demand for pools, hot tubs and beautiful locations, hen party houses can get booked up pretty quickly. Ideally, you want to book a cottage at least three months in advance.

And when to start planning

Hen party holidays are more of a commitment than a one-day event. As such, you should start planning several months in advance, so you can give everyone plenty of notice and get as many people there as possible.

With all of the above in mind, you’ll want to start planning the event around a year before the wedding takes place.

Where to have your hen party holiday

The location of a hen party holiday depends massively on who is coming.

What does the bride want?

Firstly, the bride. Does she have anywhere in mind? That could be a favourite spot from childhood holidays or an interest that lends itself to a particular location.

Brides that love the beach will adore a holiday house by the beach, for example, while thrill-seeking ladies might prefer a trip to Wales to try the world's fastest zip line.

Where is everyone based?

Then there are the other guests. It’s important to find somewhere that everyone can get to within a similar timeframe to avoid some guests feeling left out. If the bride’s future in-laws are from the other end of the country, it might be best to find a middle-point rather than making them travel much further than everyone else.

The good news is that England and Wales have so many fantastic locations for practically any type of holiday. Whether it’s coast, countryside or towns and cities with lively nightlife, you’ll find somewhere to fit.

Plan the perfect hen party holiday

At kate & tom’s, we make it easier to plan the perfect hen party holiday with a selection of stunning houses for the bride-to-be and all her guests. Search based on a range of activities and destinations. Or start by finding the perfect house, with options for 10-16, 16-20 or even 20+ hens.

If you’d like any more assistance, don’t hesitate to contact our team on hello@kateandtoms.com. Simply tell us what you’re after and we’ll see what we can do!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Complete Hen Party Holiday Guide | kate & tom’s
If you want the ultimate celebration for a bride-to-be, it doesn’t get better than a hen party holiday. Our guide covers ideas, timings, locations and more.

item.title: Dog-Friendly Staycations - A Dog Isn’t Just for Christmas
item.dc:creator: luisa
item.content:encoded:
For many of us, our dogs are more than just pets – they're cherished members of the family. The latest statistics from the World Animal Foundation reveal that more than 30% of UK households own a dog. That’s a LOT of happy pups! Dog ownership is incredibly rewarding, but it does come with responsibilities and restrictions, especially when it comes to a pet-friendly holiday. Leaving your four-legged friend behind isn't an option - that’s why dog friendly staycations are becoming an increasingly popular choice. While boarding kennels or help from friends, neighbours and relatives can be a good option when travelling, dog-friendly cottages offer a unique opportunity to bring your pal along for the fun. The increasing popularity of dog-friendly cabins in the UK alone has transformed the narrative of pet ownership. It has also unlocked a whole new sense of freedom when it comes to planning close-to-home getaways.  But how does it work? What will you need to bring on your UK holiday? Careful planning is a must when bringing your whole pack with you. In this article, we'll look into the perfect family (and dog-friendly) staycation and pet-friendly holiday cottages. There's a whole host of coastal retreats, sandy beaches and cottages with a garden your canine companion can run amok in. At kate & tom's, we've embraced this trend by offering a selection of luxurious, dog-friendly holiday homes across the UK. From grand estates to cosy cottages, there's holiday inspiration to suit every family's needs.

Dog-friendly cottages and holidays to set tails wagging...

There’s no two ways about it – pet-friendly holiday cottages represent a paradigm shift for dog owners. Even better, you may be pleasantly surprised about the standard of accommodation available. The idea that travelling with a dog means compromising on the quality of your accommodation couldn’t be further from the truth. On the contrary, luxury holiday rentals with pet-friendly policies mean you can have it all. There’s no need to settle for a rundown cottage with fur-covered furniture and a lingering ‘wet dog’ smell. Look in the right places and you’ll find a huge range of high-end, dog-friendly holiday rentals that don’t compromise on luxury. Whether it’s a sumptuous Georgian manor in the Cotswold countryside, muddy walks along the river in North Yorkshire, or even a modern barn conversion in Cumbria, a dog-friendly cottage or cabin means both you and your four-legged friends can start planning your next break as soon as you arrive.

Benefits of pet-friendly holidays

Still not convinced? Here's why a dog-friendly stay is the perfect fit for your next staycation.

1. No need for kennels

Let’s face it – the worst part of going on holiday for many pet parents is saying goodbye at the kennel or pet sitter’s front door. With a dog-friendly holiday, that heavy-hearted farewell is replaced by tail wags and happy adventures. You get the joy of watching your dog discover new sights and smells, and they get to stay close to their favourite humans – win-win!

2. Lower stress for you and your dog

Does your dog suffer from separation anxiety? Or maybe a family member isn't the biggest fan of flying? Either way, travelling abroad can be stressful – not just for you, but for your pet. It's not exactly a dog's dream, from airport queues and unfamiliar surroundings to being left behind with strangers. A UK-based staycation is a much gentler alternative. No flights, no quarantine rules, no navigating foreign pet policies. Just a straightforward journey to a dog-welcoming home-from-home – complete with plenty of walking trails and muddy paws!

3. Freedom to explore the great outdoors

One of the greatest joys of staycations is the ability to immerse yourself in the stunning countryside locations and coastline gems. Whether gathering sand in their tail at one of the UK's many dog-friendly beaches or going on many a dog walk through the rolling hills of the Lakes, dog-friendly destinations are abundant where dogs are not only allowed – they’re celebrated. The best part? You’re never too far from a great dog-friendly pub garden where you can both rest your paws and enjoy a much-deserved drink.

4. Quality time with your dog

Just like spending quality time with your loved ones, the perfect dog-friendly holiday can be a great opportunity to bond with your canine companion. From dog-friendly beaches, enclosed gardens and scenic walks on local nature trails to cosy evenings by the fireplace, you’ll love the chance to shake up your usual routine. There are many holiday cottages in the UK that you can and your canine companion can spend some time together. Don't worry, though, when your furry pal is resting, there are plenty of countryside cottages that offer hot tubs and enclosed garden spaces, perfect if you want to relax after a day of adventuring.

5. Dog-friendly holiday cottages use your budget wisely

With no flights or pet passport costs to worry about, dog-friendly holidays can be much kinder on the wallet. Many holiday homes and dog-friendly cabins offer pet-inclusive packages with everything you need for a stress-free break – think secure gardens, dog beds, bowls and even a warm welcome full of treats. Trips with your dog flip the narrative and allow you to redirect pet care expenses back into your holiday budget. Whether it’s upgrading your accommodation or splurging on memorable experiences, you’ll be amazed at how much you can save when you eliminate pet care expenses from your budget. 

6. Strengthening your bond

There’s something incredibly special about sharing a holiday with your dog. With fewer distractions and more quality time together, dog-friendly holidays can be a chance to deepen that bond, whether it’s through new walking routes, cosy nights in or simply having them snuggled up by your side as you enjoy the view. These shared experiences create lasting memories not just for you, but for your pup too.

Tips for dog-friendly staycations

With so many benefits, why leave your dog behind next time you plan a staycation for the whole family? Here are some tips to make the planning process as smooth as possible. 

1. Book early for availability at dog-friendly places

Dog-friendly accommodation is widely available in the UK but can fill up fast, especially in popular destinations. Booking your dog-friendly cottage and travelling well in advance is the best way to secure accommodation that meets your expectations, budgets and date requirements. 

2. Explore dedicated platforms

Using a dedicated search platform with a dog-friendly filter will make your search much easier. For example, kate & tom’s features a landing page dedicated exclusively to luxury dog-friendly holiday rentals. 

3. Pack thoughtfully

When planning a dog-friendly mini break, packing thoughtfully is key. Remember to bring along essentials for your dog, including their favourite toys, a familiar bed and plenty of treats. Like young kids, familiar items can help your pet settle into the new environment more comfortably.

Inspiring dog-friendly destinations

When it comes to travelling with your furry friend, England and Wales rank as some of the best destinations in the world. Here are some of the best dog-friendly places to inspire you...

1. Visit nearby pubs in the Cotswolds

With almost 800 square miles of postcard-perfect countryside at your doorstep, it’s no wonder the Cotswolds is a firm favourite with dog lovers. From bounding through ancient woodlands to swimming in lakes, strolling around chocolate-box villages and exploring arboretums, pets have just as much fun as their humans. Beyond glorious landscapes, the Cotswolds offers some of the best dog-friendly pubs in the UK. What better way to unwind with your pooch after long walks on your next dog-friendly holiday?

2. Find dog-friendly beaches on the south coast

What dog doesn’t love racing along the beach, splashing in the surf and sniffing around piles of seaweed? From Devon and Dorset to Sussex and Kent, coastal getaways are guaranteed to delight dogs and the whole family alike. Just be sure to check those summer restrictions – some beaches don't allow dogs during the busiest months. Top tip: Get a dog-friendly holiday cottage that's within walking distance of the beach, then you don't even need to get the car dirty with sand and seawater after your coastal walkies!

3. Conquer fells in the Lake District

Forget William Wordsworth and Beatrix Potter. The Lake District inspires dog owners on a whole new level. Whether you’re exploring the shores of Windermere, conquering Catbells or relaxing at a country pub, the Lake District is one of the most popular outdoor destinations in the UK for a pet-friendly holiday with the family. More specifically, the town of Keswick was voted the most dog-friendly UK town by the Kennel Club three years in a row, from 2019-2022! Take on your favourite fells in the day, then cosy up around the log burner at night. Even better, get the open fire going and enjoy sweeping views from your luxury cottage.

4. Let them roam free in North Yorkshire

From windswept beaches to rolling moorland and pup-welcoming pubs, this county truly pulls out all the stops. Start with the North York Moors National Park – miles of heather-clad hills, woodland trails and open countryside perfect for sniffing, exploring and chasing sticks to your dog’s heart’s content. The Cleveland Way offers stunning coastal walks with dramatic sea views, and much of the trail is dog-friendly. Head east to the coast and you’ll find dog-friendly beaches like Whitby (check seasonal restrictions), Sandsend and Runswick Bay, where sandy paws and salty sea air are part of the fun. After a beachy ramble, pop into one of the many pubs or cafés that happily serve up a bowl of water (and maybe a biscuit) for your pup. For a more tranquil escape, the Yorkshire Dales offer quieter villages like Reeth, Grassington and Malham, with dog-friendly cottages and plenty of walking routes right from your doorstep. So, grab the lead, pack some treats and head to North Yorkshire.

Book the best dog-friendly cottage with kate & tom's

As you learn more about dog-friendly cottages, it becomes increasingly clear the options are as diverse as the breeds themselves. From grand, heritage-listed estates hidden in the countryside to luxury lakeside houses and dog-friendly coastal cabins with a hot tub, there’s a pet-lovers retreat to suit every travel style.  One thing is for certain – the advent of dog-friendly accommodation isn’t merely a passing trend. It’s here to stay and has transformed the way pet owners approach holidays. With kate & tom’s, you can embrace a world where a dog isn't just for Christmas but a cherished part of the family and staple on family holidays. Take a look at our hand-selected dog-friendly homes across the UK for your next luxury getaway. Choose from stunning locations, secure your booking, then let the adventures begin. From one or two dogs to a whole pack, we've listed how many dogs are allowed at each of our luxury properties – but don't hesitate to get in touch if you have any questions.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: A complete guide to dog-friendly staycations, including how to choose pet-friendly accommodation, as well as tips and tricks you may not have considered.

item.title: 12 Easter Holiday Ideas for the Whole Family
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
Spring is just around the corner and along with an explosion of tulips, daffodils and bluebells comes the excitement of Easter. Filled with colour and an abundance of chocolate eggs, it’s one of the most rewarding holidays to spend with family. With two weeks of Easter holidays to play with, as well as a pair of bank holidays to stretch your annual leave, this time of year is just the ticket to plan your family getaway—and the perfect time to explore some fun and memorable Easter break holiday ideas.

Whether you want to enjoy quality time with the family by the beach or fancy horse-riding in the countryside, you can choose from several dreamy destinations perfect for young families. Have a wonderful time revelling in the magic of the Lake District or take in the spectacular scenery of the Norfolk Broads – you’ll be spoilt for choice.

There are so many amazing places at your doorstep, you don’t need to go far for your family holiday. Simply pack the car, hit the road and reach your Easter holiday in a few hours. To get you inspired we’ve put together a roundup of our favourite Easter holiday ideas for the whole family, along with tips on how to choose a crowd-pleasing luxury family holiday home to match.

1. Storybook charm in the Cotswolds

Step into a real-life storybook as you explore the idyllic landscapes of the Cotswolds. Characterised by honey-coloured cottages and rolling hills, it’s a picture-perfect destination for an Easter holiday escape. As well as chocolate-box scenery, the Cotswolds know a thing or two about how to host an eggcellent Easter. From bottle-feeding newborn lambs to Easter bunny meet ‘n’ greets, local businesses wholeheartedly embrace the family-friendly side of the holiday.

Beyond gorgeous scenery and fun-filled activities, you’ll find a cache of luxury holiday cottages in the Cotswolds. Many boast sprawling gardens and private woodlands, providing a dream backdrop for Easter egg hunts. With warm weather on the horizon, there are plenty of activities you can do outdoors – we’re thinking of a day trip exploring the area.

2. Outdoor adventures in the Lake District

For outdoorsy families with an appetite for adventure, the Lake District is one of the ultimate Easter break holiday ideas. From scenic hikes and nature walks to leisurely boat trips and steam-train rides, it’s easy to keep the entire clan entertained for days on end. The Lake District’s local businesses arrange several Easter-themed events and activities so you can do as little or as much planning as you like – there’s even loads for older children to do too.

Looking for accommodation inspiration? Spacious holiday homes in Lake District provide a perfect base for the Easter holidays or the long Easter weekend. Why not spend your days discovering this beautiful corner of Cumbria, then return to your comfortable home-from-home for a cosy evening? Many houses offer beautiful rustic fireplaces and cinema rooms where you can host a family movie night. Idyllic, right?

3. Coastal magic in Cornwall

[caption id="attachment_67545" align="alignnone" width="719"] Portrait Of Three Generation Family On Beach Holiday[/caption]

If a coastal escape is on your Easter wish list, Cornwall is calling. With its sandy beaches, rugged cliffs and charming seaside villages, it’s a destination that never fails to delight. While traditionally associated with summer getaways, Cornwall is just as lovely in the spring. Whether it’s a stylish barn conversion, stately mansion or ultra-modern beach house with sweeping sea views, you’re spoilt for choice when it comes to Spring holiday luxury holiday rentals in Cornwall.

Pro tip for Easter break holiday ideas: consider what types of activities your family enjoys and choose a holiday rental to match. For example, if you’re a family of tennis lovers why not book a property with a private court and host your very own Easter tournament?

4. Tranquil retreats in the Norfolk Broads

If R&R is on your radar this Easter, then why not take a road trip to the Norfolk Broads? A popular destination filled with serene rivers and lakes, you can spend hours discovering one of the largest protected wetland areas in Britain. Whether you’re exploring local nature reserves or hitting up family-friendly attractions like petting zoos and theme parks, there’s plenty to keep you entertained in this pocket of East Anglia.

Sometimes so much action can wear you and the children out. So, it might be best to lay your head in a relaxing space you can call home during your stay. Take your pick from beautiful barns with a games room or a Norfolk country manor with a tennis court. There are so many to choose from!

5. Easter escapades in the Peak District

A landscape of hills, dales and moorlands makes the Peak District an outdoor playground for adventure enthusiasts. From organised egg hunts at local farmyards and ranger-led activities at wildlife parks to outdoor concerts and colourful craft activities at National Trust properties, the Peak District calendar is packed with Easter-themed events. It’s no wonder the Peak District is a firm favourite on the kate & tom’s Easter holiday ideas list. You’re guaranteed a wonderful time.

Need a rest day after all the action? Spacious living areas, chef-inspired kitchens and luxury facilities like hot tubs and indoor pools make it easy to spend an entire day relaxing at your Peak District holiday cottage.

6. Enjoy the warm weather with seaside breaks in Devon

 

Devon’s sandy shores and kid-friendly atmosphere make it an ideal Easter retreat. Is your guest list quickly eggs-panding? Choose a holiday home in Devon with enough bedrooms for everyone.

Whether it’s a spacious kitchen for cooking family-style meals or a sprawling garden to help the kids burn off steam, the luxury of having your own space will make your family holiday all the more enjoyable.

7. Relaxing retreats in the Yorkshire Dales

After the chaos of Christmas, many Brits prefer to keep their Easter holidays low-key. If you’re craving a fix of nature and relaxation, look no further than the Yorkshire Dales. With stunning views, you won’t want to miss and an array of outdoor activities for the kids, the Dales is the ideal option for a pared-back (but super fun) half-term break.

From beautifully restored manor houses complete with hot tubs, games rooms and gyms to country estates set in flower-filled gardens, luxury holiday rentals in the Yorkshire Dales make it easy to create your own Easter entertainment. And of course, when you’re ready for a change of scene, God’s Own Country is at your doorstep.

8. Stunning surroundings in the South

There’s no denying the coast of Wales is somewhat magical. With impressive beaches you can visit whatever the weather to picturesque views that welcome you and your family every day, Wales is the perfect destination for your next trip. Choose from Snowdonia and Anglesey in the north, or head to Pembrokeshire in the south – wherever you decide you’ll have no regrets.

Spend two weeks in a luxury coastal cottage in Wales. You’ll need somewhere to relax after action-packed days spent exploring the local scenery and coast. Treat the family (and you) to a coastal hideaway by the beach. Or, why not stay in a real-life converted barn with a pool, hot tub, and cinema room? The possibilities are endless.

9. Say "yes" to days by the sea in Sussex

How can you say “no” to views that leave you speechless and long-stretching beaches? We know we couldn't. Sussex plays host to some of the UK’s most popular seaside resorts, whether it’s Chichester or Camber Sands, this iconic destination is a no-brainer.

Explore the beautiful South Downs, or head to Hastings if you fancy adding a bit of history to your visit – either way, Sussex has a wide range of fantastic ideas for everyone. Make your visit even more memorable by staying at a wacky hideaway brimming with eccentric interior design. We like the sound of that.

10. Take to the city of Liverpool

Take a break from the norm and take a long Easter weekend in the North West. Liverpool is a historic city that offers loads of fun for the entire family. Take older kids on a tour to discover the history of The Beatles at the Cavern Club. Going with younger children? Don’t worry, there’s plenty to do in Liverpool with smaller kids. Head to Croxteth Park Riding Centre or take them to an aqua adventure park a stone’s throw away from the city centre.

As for where to stay during your trip to Liverpool? A modern Georgian townhouse in a super central location is always a good idea. Or enjoy a luxury family holiday in a beautiful apartment in the heart of the city.

11. Have the best time in Bath

You might think Bath is just for short romantic retreats or long weekends away with friends, on the contrary, Bath is the perfect half-term destination for you and your brood. Potter around town before stopping for some cake at one of the Bath’s many quaint tea rooms. Fancy a bit of history? This iconic location is brimming with educational tours and sites like Bath Abbey and The Roman Baths.

If you’re little ‘uns are looking for more adventure, there’s zip-lining at local parks or clay pigeon shooting at Coombs Clay Shooting for older kids. You can also discover the caverns at nearby Wookey Hole Caves – see? Something for everyone. Make your getaway even more luxe by staying in a magnificent Bath townhouse.

12. Discover the idyllic Isle of Wight

Want both countryside and beaches? The Isle of Wight is the perfect blend of island life and serene landscapes. Take your children on a fossil hunt on the miles and miles of coastline the Isle of Wight has to offer, or go wild at one of the UK’s oldest theme parks. Either way, you’ll have a time you’ll never forget. As for the grown-ups? There’s plenty to do if you want to take some time for yourself. Go shopping in Newport or Cowes, or if the weather’s warmer, go for a sunbathe on the beach.

Enjoy your Easter holidays at a beautiful manor with a roaring fire and heated pool while watching the sunset over the Quay.

Make the most of your Easter break with luxury family holidays

Whether you’re dreaming of an escape to the country, a seaside getaway or even a city break, luxury holiday rentals from kate & tom’s provide the ultimate base for a family-friendly Easter half-term. Instead of checking into separate hotel rooms or B&Bs, imagine settling into a spacious property with dedicated spaces for all generations, as well as plenty of common ground to bond, connect and create memories.

Choose from a swanky townhouse in the heart of Liverpool, an oasis in the Isle of Wight, a converted barn in Wales, or even a castle in Sussex – whatever you pick you won’t be disappointed.

Need help planning your dream Easter getaway? Get in touch with the dedicated team at kate & tom’s. Email hello@kateandtoms.com to find out more about our favourite Easter break holiday ideas, as well as handpicked holiday rentals.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: 12 Easter Break Holiday Ideas for the Whole Family | kate & tom’s
A family-oriented guide to Easter break holiday ideas in the UK, including coastal retreats and nature-filled adventures.

item.title: Where to Go on Holiday in May – UK Edition
item.dc:creator: luisa
item.content:encoded:
Wondering where to go on holiday in May? Don’t miss our curated guide to the best spring getaway destinations. Whether you’re dreaming of sun, sand and saltwater or country charm, there’s something for everyone in our roundup. 

Embrace the arts in Norfolk 

From classic music concerts to cabaret acts, The Norfolk & Norwich Festival sees the region come alive with a kaleidoscope of performances. More than 250 years in the making, the event attracts tens of thousands of festivalgoers every season. The countryside is peppered with stunning self-contained rental properties, making Norfolk a dream destination for a creativity-fuelled getaway.  

Beat the summer crowds in Cornwall

With average highs of 14°C and plenty of sunshine, Cornwall is surprisingly pleasant in May. It’s not exactly sunbathing weather, but the cooler temperatures are ideal for scenic beach walks and rambles through the surrounding moorlands. Stroll around charming harbour villages like Falmouth, explore historic landmarks like Tintagel Castle and catch a show at the Minack Theatre, one of the most atmospheric performing arts venues in England.  Weather not cooperating? Not to worry. You’re never too far from a tearoom in Cornwall. Settle in and devour a scrumptious platter of scones, jam and clotted cream served with a freshly brewed pot of tea. Don’t forget to slather on the jam first followed by a dollop of cream – this all-important order is what sets Cornish cream teas apart from their Devonshire counterparts. 

Celebrate music and literature in Bath

Now in its 76th year, The Bath Festival is a citywide celebration of music and literature. Discover an extraordinary programme of events for all ages, from family-friendly concerts to book signings. The Cotswolds is at your doorstep in Bath, so don’t forget your walking shoes!

Stay dry in Bognor Regis

With just over 40mm of rainfall, May is one of the driest months of the year in Bognor Regis. Tucked away in West Sussex, this seaside gem is a favourite with Londoners in search of R&R on May Day Bank Holiday. It’ll probably be a little chilly to swim but there’s no shortage of chocolate-box villages, quaint market towns and centuries-old castles to explore. South Downs National Park is just 20 minutes away by car, making it easy to stretch your legs and discover a landscape of rolling hills, ancient woodlands and flower-strewn heathland. 

Old-fashioned fun in Gloucestershire

Looking for unique ideas for where to go in May half term? Set your sights on Gloucestershire, home to the annual Tetbury Woolsack Race. Held on the last bank holiday Monday in May, the event dates back to the 17th century. Cheer on competitors as they race along one of the steepest streets in town, carrying 60lb woolsacks! The race itself isn’t the only event on the agenda, with a street fair, pop-up stalls and amusement rides to keep you entertained. 

Get a dose of Vitamin D in Eastbourne

Wondering where to go in the UK in May for sunshine? A huge seven hours of daily sunshine makes Eastbourne one of England’s sunniest destinations in May. Fringed by miles of pristine beaches, the Victorian resort town is the perfect place for a spring retreat. There’s plenty to keep you entertained if rainy weather sets in, including the fantastic Towner Art Gallery filled with modern British masterpieces and Redoubt Fortress, a Napoleonic stronghold reimagined as a military museum. 

Get jazzy in Cheltenham 

Celebrate the arrival of spring at the annual Cheltenham Jazz Festival. One of the most anticipated jazz events in the UK, the festival attracts celebrated musicians from around the world. When it comes to accommodation you’re spoilt for choice. From Regency townhouses in the heart of Cheltenham to luxurious manor houses in the Cotswolds countryside, self-contained holiday rentals are the perfect way to set the tone for your May getaway. Pro tip – stock up on ingredients and use your chef’s kitchen to whip up Jazz Age cocktails like the French 75, Southside and Sidecar. 

Cheer on cyclists in Lincoln

More than just a pretty face, Lincoln hosts one of the most exciting cycling events on the British sports calendar. Every year cyclists take to the streets of the Cathedral Quarter for the annual Lincoln Grand Prix, a two-day road bike race that kicks off in Castle Square. Cheer on competitors as they ascend Michaelgate, an impossibly steep cobbled street with a calf-burning gradient of 20%. If you’re wondering where to go in the UK in May for sporting events, Lincolnshire is a top choice.

Escape to the country in Nottinghamshire

What better way to usher in spring than with a quintessential British county show? The Nottinghamshire County Show is one of our favourites and celebrates the region’s agricultural roots with a scrum of events. Admire ribbon-winning livestock, watch equine shows, sample food from local producers and shop for one-of-a-kind gifts at craft stalls. Don’t forget your tweeds!

Get salty at the Salcombe Crab Festival

Love seafood and wondering where to go on holiday in May? You’ll fall shell over claws for the annual Salcombe Crab Festival. A staple on the Devon spring events calendar, this fishy festival pays tribute to the famous ‘Cancer Pagurus’ brown crabs caught off the coast of Devon. Renowned for their soft flesh and sweet flavour, the crustaceans are so delicious they’ve earned their very own festival. It’s held in the adorable seaside town of Salcombe, where you’ll find some beautiful holiday cottages. Some even boast uninterrupted sea views, complete with heated outdoor pools, sun-drenched terraces and gardens that cascade down to the water. 

Book your spring getaway with kate & tom’s

With so many amazing destinations to choose from, choosing where to go on holiday for May half term has never been more fun. Whether you’re craving a seaside getaway in Cornwall, a music-filled extravaganza in Cheltenham or a fix of fresh air in the Cotswolds, find luxury May holiday rentals for every occasion with kate & tom’s.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Where to Go on Holiday in May: UK Edition | kate & tom’s
Whether you’re dreaming of sun, sand and saltwater or country charm, don’t miss our curated guide to where to go on holiday in May.

item.title: 5 Summer Solstice Events & Celebrations in the UK
item.dc:creator: brett
item.content:encoded:
Summer solstice events are a great way to celebrate that summer is well and truly here. There are plenty to choose from, with people gathering up and down the country on the longest day of the year. Read on as we explore what happens and pick out five of the best summer solstice celebrations the UK has to offer.

What exactly is summer solstice?

Summer solstice marks the ‘longest day’ of the year, accompanied by the ‘shortest night’. Of course, there’s no change to the usual 24 hours on the clock. It simply refers to the day which has the most hours and minutes of sunlight, and the night that follows. Solstice comes from the words ‘sol’ and ‘sistere’, which mean sun and stand still, respectively. Reflecting this, the solstice is about taking a moment to consider the transition from days getting lighter to days getting darker. In that moment, you can celebrate what you’ve achieved in the past half-year and look forward to what the next six months holds. For summer solstice celebrations, UK locations typically get between 16.5-17.5 hours of sunlight. In the northern hemisphere, it occurs on June 21st or June 20th each year. This date marks the middle of summer, or ‘midsummer’ for many countries. Traditionally, it’s been celebrated with themes of nourishment, fertility, brightness and power. Or maybe you just think it’s great that summer is here! With that in mind, it’s no surprise that getting outdoors is the order of the day. Most summer solstice events revolve around the particularly early sunrise or particularly late sunset. However, there are also activities that simply involve traditional rituals throughout the day…

5 Summer solstice events

Stonehenge

It will come as no surprise to see Stonehenge at the top of our list of summer solstice celebrations in the UK. The iconic stone circle in Wiltshire is open to the public for free on summer solstice day, when people gather to watch the sunrise. It’s believed that the stones were built with this in mind – aligning with the sunrise on solstice days. Along with the Winter Solstice, it’s one of only two days of the year when you can see the stones up close. Normally, visitors are kept around 15 yards from the circle. It’s easily the most famous summer solstice event, with around 10,000 people gathering each year.

Durdle Door

Above all else, people want somewhere spectacular to see the sunrise on the longest day of the year. While Stonehenge might hold more cultural significance, Durdle Door certainly has the upper hand when it comes to natural beauty. Situated on Dorset’s Jurassic Coast, the unique limestone arch was formed by the power of the waves thousands of years ago. Each year, people gather on the beach and hills to see the sunrise with the iconic stone arch in view. With the right position on the beach, you can see the sun rise directly through the door for a particularly iconic Instagram snap! Don’t stress too much about finding the right spot though – the sun rises pretty slowly, so you’ll have plenty of time to move around.

Little Moreton Hall

If you want to get better acquainted with the traditional meaning of summer solstice celebrations, head to Little Moreton Hall in Cheshire. The National Trust property comprises a wood-frame house with an iconic Tudor wood frame, plus a surrounding moat and knot gardens. For the summer solstice, the Hall hosts midsummer celebrations in true Tudor style. The festival focuses on planting crops and creating decorations. There’s also live music with instruments from bygone years and puppet parades for all the family to enjoy.

Arbor Low or Castlerigg

Who said Stonehenge is the only stone circle in the UK? If you’re based up north or just don’t fancy the huge Wiltshire crowds, there are other stone circles for a similarly spiritual summer solstice experience. Try saying that quickly! Arbor Low is known as the Peak District’s Stonehenge, located just outside of Bakewell. Like Stonehenge, its origins aren’t completely confirmed – but it appears to be a ceremonial site, making it ideal for solstice in the summer. It’s situated on high moorland, meaning you can see the horizon for miles in every direction. Or venture to Castlerigg Stone Circle in Cumbria – around a mile and a half outside of Keswick. It’s made up of 38 stones, thought to hold ceremonial significance, but again with unknown origins. The unique selling point here are the beautiful Lake District Fells surrounding the stone circle, which you can explore after sunrise or in the days that follow your summer solstice celebrations.

Glastonbury Tor

While Glastonbury might be better known for its spiritual experiences of a different kind, the town is also home to one of the best summer solstice events. Around five miles away from the festival site, much closer to the town of Glastonbury, you’ll find Glastonbury Tor – a steep but moderately sized hill that rises 500 feet above the Somerset surroundings. The hill itself has an unusual shape due to artificial terraces – the origins of which are unknown. Sensing a theme? This is also another summer solstice site with spiritual significance for both Pagans and Christians. That’s partly due to the tower of the church of St Michael, which sits at the top of the hill.

Enjoy the best summer solstice celebrations

From stone circles and beautiful landmarks to traditional Tudor homes, the UK has a variety of summer solstice events to choose from. Whichever you prefer, kate & tom’s can provide the luxury holiday home for a truly spectacular solstice getaway. Our large houses sleep anywhere from 10 to 40+ guests in the most sought-after locations across England and Wales. That includes Wiltshire, Somerset, Dorset in the south along with Cheshire, Cumbria and the Peak District in the north. With hot tubs, swimming pools, tennis courts and games rooms to choose from, you can make summer solstice celebrations that little bit more special.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: 5 Summer Solstice Events & Celebrations in the UK | kate & tom's
Enjoy the ‘longest day’ of the year with the best summer solstice celebrations and events, from iconic stone circle sunrises to a traditional Tudorfestival.

item.title: Your Must-See UK Events Calendar for 2024
item.dc:creator: maryam
item.content:encoded:
If there's one thing that unites Brits, it's a shared love for celebrating. From music festivals to cultural events, the United Kingdom hosts a smorgasbord of experiences throughout the year.

Want to stay on top of the most anticipated UK events in 2024? Keep reading for our UK events calendar:

Bath reBalance Festival, January 25th – February 18th

Feeling a little off balance after the festive season? Rest, rejuvenate and reset at reBalance Bath – The Festival of Wellbeing. The perfect excuse for a girls’ getaway, the festival features three weeks of events designed to nurture the mind, body and soul. And of course, it never hurts to have your own private Bath holiday cottage complete with wellness-inspired facilities like heated pools and hot tubs. Sweeping views over the Somerset countryside are the icing on the cake.

The Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race, April 7th

As spring blossoms along the Thames, the Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race steps up as a highlight of the British sporting UK events calendar. The race unfolds in London though the celebrations continue back in Oxford and Cambridge, where you’ll find some truly stunning holiday rental properties – perfect for hosting post-race parties.   

Grand National, April 11th – 13th

Held at the illustrious Aintree Racecourse in Liverpool, the Grand National is one of the most anticipated horse races in the world. It sees competitors complete two circuits of the famous Grand National course, which features dozens of fences. The meeting spans for three days though let’s be honest, Ladies’ Day steals the spotlight and is one of the most anticipated UK events in 2024. This year it’s scheduled for April 12, and you can bet Aintree will be overrun with colourful frocks and fascinators.

The Chelsea Flower Show, May 21st – 25th

Held in the heart of London, the annual Chelsea Flower Show transforms the Royal Hospital into a spectacular floral extravaganza. The event itself is packed to the brim but when it comes to sightseeing in London, spring is a fantastic time to beat the crowds. Looking for accommodation in the capital? Swap cramped urban hotels for luxury holiday cottages in the countryside.

Hay Festival, May 23rd – June 2nd

Every year, literature enthusiasts flock to Wales for the world-famous Hay Festival. Held in the charming town of Hay-on-Wye, the festival is nicknamed the “Woodstock of the mind” and aims to inspire, educate and spark meaningful conversations. This year the programme is more dynamic than ever, making the Hay Festival one of our top UK events in 2024. Beyond books, the staggeringly beautiful landscapes of Brecon Beacons National Park are at your doorstep in Hay-on-Wye, so why not tag on a few extra days to explore the region? By the coast, destinations like Tenby and Aberystwyth beckon with postcard-perfect seascapes.

Glastonbury Festival, June 26th – 30th

Glastonbury Festival needs no introduction. As one of the most iconic music festivals in the world, it draws crowds from far and wide. In 2024, rumours are swirling that iconic artists like Coldplay, Madonna and the Spice Girls will headline the event.

While the muddy fields of Worthy Farm are a haven for music lovers, luxury holiday rentals in Somerset elevate the Glastonbury experience to the next level. After a day of revelry, there’s no better way to unwind than with a hot tub soak under the Somerset stars and a good night’s sleep in a plush bed.

Taylor Swift: The Eras Tour, June & August

If you were lucky enough to score tickets to The Eras Tour, you’ll know just how big the event is. In 2024, the cinematic tour will touch down in the UK, hitting London, Edinburgh, Liverpool and Cardiff. Whatever city you’re headed to, why not make your trip shimmer with a luxury holiday rental with enough space to accommodate your entire Swifty crew?

Notting Hill Carnival, August 25th – 26th

 

The infectious rhythms of the Caribbean take over the streets of West London during the Notting Hill Carnival. Without a doubt, it’s one of the most colourful instalments on the UK events calendar. The atmosphere is electric but can leave you exhausted, which is where luxury holiday rentals in London step up. After hours of dancing, return to your urban sanctuary for well-deserved R&R.

Diwali – October 24th

Looking for family-friendly UK events in 2024? Diwali, also known as the Festival of Lights, is celebrated across the country and never fails to dazzle. If you’re not familiar with the event, it symbolises the "victory of light over darkness, good over evil and knowledge over ignorance".

As well as spiritual significance for Hindus, Jains and Sikhs, the festival is embraced by Brits across the country with parties, fireworks and flickering candles. Diwali is all about celebrating with family and friends, making it the perfect time to plan a group getaway.

Bonfire Night – November 5th

 

Wrap up the year with an explosive Bonfire Night celebrated with family and friends. Forget disappointing local displays. There are plenty of places throughout England and Wales that really go the extra mile.

Why not head to Lewes in Sussex – known to many as the bonfire capital of the world! Their lively celebrations include a flaming barrel run, effigies being marched through the streets and topical firework-packed models created by local societies.
